experiment: F-BRN4 hippocampal indexing at 2.5x scale — sawtooth degradation pattern
expect: INDEX.md coverage degraded from 98.7% (S301 at 307L) to 85-90% at 779L. At least 1 bucket >40L. Domain INDEXes remain unwired.
method: Counted lesson files (memory/lessons/L-*.md), parsed INDEX.md theme table counts, computed coverage percentage, bucket distribution, dark matter growth, and scaling projections. Cross-referenced with S189, S301, S353 baselines for time series.

baselines.note: Before remediation. INDEX.md barely indexes 72% of corpus.
Major bucket split 19→21 themes. F-BRN4 declared resolved.

coverage.dark_matter_composition: 48 lessons post-L-809, minus 5 that were added to existing themes

meta_concentration.interpretation: Hippocampal volume bias — meta-knowledge dominates the index

domain_index_gap.l601_pattern: Norm-level recommendation → 0% adoption (P-241: execute within opening session or abandon)

explicit_references.interpretation: INDEX.md is a theme-count summary with representative examples, not a complete reference

retrieval_proxy.driver: orient.py and dispatch_optimizer.py drive sessions, not INDEX.md direct lookup

sawtooth_pattern.description: Coverage degrades as lessons accumulate, then remediation (bucket splitting) restores it. Cycle repeats.
sawtooth_pattern.hippocampal_isomorphism: Degradation-remediation cycle = hippocampal reconsolidation. Dark matter = cortical representations not yet consolidated by hippocampal replay. Bucket splitting = memory reorganization during sleep-like consolidation.

diff: Expected 85-90% coverage — got 94.5% (BETTER than predicted). Expected bucket overflow — FALSIFIED (max 36). Expected domain INDEX gap — CONFIRMED (0/43 after 102 sessions). SURPRISE: sawtooth pattern — coverage is cyclical not monotonic. Dark matter grows 4.3x faster than lessons (super-linear) but periodic remediation resets it. The hippocampal reconsolidation isomorphism is validated: the swarm DOES require periodic reorganization, and without it, retrieval degrades.
key_findings: Sawtooth degradation: coverage oscillates between remediation highs (~95-99%) and degradation lows (~76-83%) as lesson corpus grows
Dark matter growth is super-linear: 4→43→86→43 (not monotonic — remediation-dependent)
Meta concentration 46.5%: hippocampal volume bias toward self-referential knowledge
Domain INDEX recommendation abandoned after 102 sessions (L-601 norm-level prediction confirmed)
Hippocampal reconsolidation isomorphism validated: periodic reorganization is necessary and structurally analogous to sleep consolidation
next_remediation: Bucket split needed at ~1280L (projected 90% coverage threshold) or when max bucket hits 40 (~1408L)
